zh.Hull, Yorkshire, England: 英國英格蘭越可爾赫爾
zh.lifeSummary: Judith Robinson是Anthony Robinson和Mrs. Anthony Robinson的女兒，於1320年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾金斯頓 武朋 赫爾。她於1339年與Hugh Dykensonne在英國英格蘭越可爾金斯頓 武朋 赫爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1376年在英國英格蘭越可爾赫爾享年56歲。

nameMeanings.name: Robinson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): patronymic from the Middle English personal name Robin , a pet form of Robert , + -son. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
French: from a pet form of the personal name   Robin  .

West Indian (including Haiti) and Guyanese: most likely not (only) of English or French origin as in 1 above and 2 above, but also, if not mostly, from the related name of the famous Daniel Defoe's literary character Robinson Crusoe (from a novel first published in 1719).

variants.name: Robbinson
meanings: Altered form of English or French   Robinson  .

variants.name: Robin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Andre, Emile, Marcel, Achille, Armand, Arnaud, Camille, Julien, Michel, Normand.
French, Breton, Scottish, English, and German: from the personal name Robin, a pet form of Robert , composed of the short form Rob + the hypocoristic suffix -in.
Slovenian: topographic name derived from rob ‘edge, border’ (see Robar ).

nameMeanings.name: Judith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, meaning ‘Jewess’ or ‘woman from Judea’, borne by a Jewish heroine whose story is recorded in the Book of Judith in the Apocrypha. Judith is portrayed as a beautiful widow who delivers her people from the invading Assyrians by gaining the confidence of their commander, Holofernes, and cutting off his head while he is asleep; without their commander, the Assyrians are duly routed. This has been a perennially popular Jewish name. In the English-speaking world it was taken up in the 16th century, having been in occasional use among Gentiles before this: for example, it was borne by a niece of William the Conqueror. It enjoyed great popularity between the 1940s and the 1960s. Today's notable bearers include the American novelist  Judith Krantz  (  b. 1928 ) and the Scottish composer  Judith Weir  (  b. 1954 ).

en.lifeSummary: Judith Robinson was born in 1320, in Kingston upon Hull, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom as the daughter of Anthony Robinson and Mrs. Anthony Robinson. She married Hugh Dykensonne in 1339, in Kingston upon Hull, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1376, in Hull, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 56.

ko.Kingston Upon Hull, Yorkshire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 킨그스턴 업 훌
ko.lifeSummary: Judith Robinson(은)는 1320년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 킨그스턴 업 훌에서 Anthony Robinson(와)과 Mrs. Anthony Robinson의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1339년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 킨그스턴 업 훌에서 Hugh Dykensonne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1376년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 훌에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.

Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hugh Dykensonne в 1339 г. в местности кингстон упон хулл, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына.
